current.summary: Install safety strategy complete — quarantine fetch, behavioral scanner, health signals with caching, dependency auto-install. Ready for implementation planning.

activeWork.description: Install safety system for syllago — facts-only, zero-governance approach to content verification at install time
activeWork.progress: Strategy doc is comprehensive and final. Covers: quarantine fetch (using existing StagingDir), content hashing, native Go behavioral scanner + optional YARA via CLI, git+platform health signals with per-content granularity, cached signal store, dependency auto-install. Signal design includes gaming resistance tiers, two-tier display (registry vs content), content-type risk tiers, and anti-pattern flags. Needs implementation plan → beads → execute.
blockers: Needs implementation plan broken into tasks before execution can begin
Open question: exact content hash algorithm (normalization rules, blanking regex)
Open question: platform API rate limiting strategy (GitHub token support)

git.lastCommit: 3b34379 handoff: CDR execution complete, content-based classification gap identified

recentDecisions: Facts-only system: no social trust, no vouches, no denouncement network, no governance layer
Behavioral scanner is native Go pattern matching (no CGO), YARA via CLI as optional enhancement
Two-tier signal display: registry-level context + per-content-item primary signals
Content-type risk tiers: executable (hooks/scripts) > external-pointing (MCP) > instructional (skills/agents) > passive (rules)
Signal cache at ~/.local/share/syllago/signals/, refreshes with existing registryAutoSync preference
Dependency auto-install at first run (bwrap, socat required; yara optional)
UX language: 'no known issues detected' never 'clean' or 'safe'; publish scan categories not exact patterns
Quarantine fetch using existing StagingDir infrastructure — files never in agent-visible dirs until user approves
nextSteps: Create implementation plan from strategy doc (break into phases/tasks)
Phase 1 likely: native Go behavioral scanner (highest standalone value, zero deps)
Phase 2 likely: quarantine fetch integration into install flow
Phase 3 likely: health signals collection + caching
Commit the 3 new research files (16, 17, 18) before starting implementation

context.notes: This handoff is for the IMPLEMENTATION of the install safety strategy. The parent session is continuing to work on provenance/attestation spec design (separate concern). The strategy doc (file 18) is the definitive reference — it went through 2 adversarial panels, vouch/web-of-trust research, security scanning landscape research, and multiple rounds of simplification. Key files: 16 (social trust — killed), 17 (community signals — superseded), 18 (final strategy). Files 01-15 are research background. The existing sandbox package (cli/internal/sandbox/) has bwrap, staging dir, egress proxy already built — the quarantine fetch reuses this infrastructure.
